'''Théodore Pascal Fages''' (born 23 August 1994) is a French professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh}} and {{rlp|so}} for [[Baroudeurs de Pia XIII|Pia]] in the [[Super XIII]] and [[France national rugby league team|France]] at international level.

He previously played for the [[Salford Red Devils]], [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Player | website=Betfred Super League | date=2 March 2023 | url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/404 | access-date=28 August 2023}}</ref> He played as a {{rlp|hk}} earlier in his career.{{citation needed|date=August 2023}}

==Background==
He is the son of former French international [[Pascal Fages]] who appeared for France in the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup]].{{citation needed|date=August 2023}} Théo replicated his father's achievements in playing for France at a World Cup when he captained his nation at the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].{{citation needed|date=August 2023}}

==Club career==
===Salford Red Devils===
Fages joined [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] as a 16-year-old, progressing through the club's academy system. He made his [[Super League]] début for Salford in the first match of the 2013's [[Super League XVIII]] in a defeat by [[Wigan Warriors]]; the following week he played against [[Catalans Dragons]] and was knocked unconscious in the opening minutes of the match by [[Julian Bousquet]], which resulted in a red card for the Catalan player.<ref>[http://www.walesonline.co.uk/sport/other-sport/rugby-league-dragons-edge-over-1908903 Rugby league: Dragons have edge over Crusaders ] "From kick-off it was 100-0 after Théo Fages forced his way over and Thibaut Cordoba converted."</ref>
[[File:Fages salford.jpg|thumb|right|Fages preparing to make a tackle for Salford in 2015]]
In 2015, Fages submitted a transfer request to leave Salford, with Salford owner [[Marwan Koukash]] demanding a world record transfer fee for the player.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/jun/08/theo-fages-wants-move-salford-world-record-fee-marwan-koukash|title=Theo Fages wants move but Salford owner will demand world-record fee|agency=Press Association|date=8 June 2015|accessdate=8 July 2023|newspaper=The Guardian}}</ref>

===St Helens===
On Tuesday 22 September, it was announced that Fages would be released from the Salford club, following receipt of an undisclosed transfer fee. Two days later it was announced that he would join [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] from 2016.

On 3 June 2019 Fages signed a contract extension keeping him at St Helens until 2021.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/34336910|title=Salford's Fages joins St Helens|work=BBC Sport |accessdate=8 July 2023}}</ref>

Fages played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|publisher=Mirror|date=24 August 2019|accessdate=1 November 2019}}</ref>

Fages played in the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Salford Red Devils]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/12/st-helens-vs-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford|publisher=Telegraph|date=12 October 2019|accessdate=25 October 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens 23-6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|website=The Guardian|date=12 October 2019|last1=Unwin|first1=Will}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11777123/st-helens-win-2019-super-league-league-leaders-shield/|title=St Helens win 2019 Super League League Leaders' shield|website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

Fages scored his first [[drop goal]] for St Helens in the [[Golden point]] win against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] on 11 September 2020.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54073460|title=Super League: St Helens 21-20 Hull Kingston Rovers - Theo Fages' drop-goal sent St Helens back to the top of Super League with a golden-point extra-time win against Hull Kingston Rovers|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=12 Sep 2020}}</ref>

Fages played in St Helens 8-4 [[2020 Super League Grand Final]] victory over Wigan at the [[Kingston Communications Stadium]] in Hull.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>

Fages played for St. Helens in their [[2021 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over Castleford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-celebrate-challenge-cup-21081897/|title=How St Helens players have celebrated their first Challenge Cup win in 13 years|website=www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants===
On the 13 October 2021, it was confirmed that Fages would be joining [[Huddersfield Giants]] from 2022.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/19645313.st-helens-theo-fages-signs-huddersfield-giants//|title=St Helens' Theo Fages signs for Huddersfield Giants|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk|date=13 October 2021 }}</ref>

Fages made his club debut for Huddersfield in round 1 of the 2022 Super League season where they defeated [[Toulouse Olympique]] 42-14.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/toulouse-olympique-wont-be-easy-pickings-insists-giants-wardle-3568479/|title=Toulouse Olympique won't be easy pickings for any one in Super League, says Huddersfield Giants' Jake Wardle|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk|date=15 February 2022 }}</ref>

On 11 August 2023, Fages signed a two-year deal to join the [[Catalans Dragons]] ahead of the 2024 Super League season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/theo-fages-catalans-dragons-move-confirmed-as-huddersfield-giants-star-targets-trophies-in-france/|title=Theo Fages: Catalans Dragons move confirmed as Huddersfield Giants star targets trophies in France|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

===Catalans Dragons===
Fages made his club debut for Catalans in round 1 of the 2024 Super League season against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]. The following week, he scored two tries for Catalans in their 34-0 victory over the newly promoted [[London Broncos]] side.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68337683|title=Super League: London Broncos 0-34 Catalans Dragons - visitors cruise to win|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

=== Pia XIII ===
In August 2025, it was announced that Fages would be returning to his childhood club, [[Baroudeurs de Pia XIII]], for the 2025/2026 season. Pia play in the French [[Super XIII]] competition. On 6 June 2026, Fages scored the [[golden point]] drop goal for Pia in their 31–20 win over Carcassonne in the Super XIII grand final.<ref name=TM6jun2026>{{cite web|url=https://treizemondial.fr/pia-est-champion-de-france-apres-sa-victoire-epique-face-a-carcassonne/ |title=Pia is French champion after her epic victory against Carcassonne |website=Treize Mondial |date=6 June 2026 |access-date=7 June 2026}}</ref>

==International career==
Fages made his début for [[France national rugby league team|France]] in the victory over [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] at the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]], and went on to make one further appearance in the tournament.

He played in the [[2014 European Cup]], scoring his first try for France in a victory over [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]].

He was named captain of the French team prior to the [[2015 European Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/13873290.theo-fages-to-skipper-france-in-european-championship-opener-against-ireland/|title=Saints recruit Theo Fages to skipper France in European Championship opener against Ireland|date=15 October 2015|website=St Helens Star|accessdate=8 July 2023}}</ref> In the tournament he scored against [[Ireland]] (1 try).
